Risk Title: QA Budget Overrun with High Defect Leakage
Risk Description: QA budget exceeded planned allocation by ₹25,000 (8.3% overrun) due to increased retesting cycles. Multiple defects required 2-3 retest cycles with reopen counts reaching 2 per defect, indicating incomplete fixes and quality issues. This pattern suggests inadequate initial testing coverage and potential technical debt accumulation.
Impact: Budget overrun impacts project profitability and indicates quality control gaps requiring immediate attention. Continued defect leakage may delay delivery and increase technical debt.

Risk Title: DevOps Budget Overrun Due to Production Monitoring
Risk Description: DevOps budget exceeded planned allocation by ₹20,000 (10% overrun) due to increased production monitoring requirements and environment dependency issues. Access permission delays for UAT/prod environments caused bottlenecks in integration validation and log analysis activities.
Impact: Budget overrun indicates underestimated infrastructure complexity. Environment access delays create cascading impacts on delivery timelines and increase operational costs.

SLA Metric: PR approval delays causing cycle time increase
Risk Title: PR Approval Bottleneck Causing Delivery Delays
Risk Description: PR approval delays consistently impacted bug fix closure timelines across all 8 sprints. Multiple tasks remained in 'Blocked' or 'In Progress' status waiting for architecture reviewer approvals, causing cycle time increases and preventing QA from completing retesting cycles. This created a cascading delay effect throughout the delivery pipeline.
Impact: PR approval delays create bottlenecks in delivery pipeline, extend defect resolution time, and increase overall project cycle time. This impacts team velocity and stakeholder confidence.

Risk Description: Recurring environment access permission delays blocked multiple tasks across all sprints. UAT/prod-like environment access requests faced approval delays, impacting integration validation, data flow checks, and production monitoring activities. Dependency tracking tasks frequently remained in 'Blocked' status due to external approval requirements.
Impact: Environment access delays create critical path bottlenecks, prevent timely testing and validation, and increase project risk. This impacts delivery predictability and team productivity.

SLA Metric: Developers 82% vs planned 70%, QA 24% vs planned 20%
Risk Description: Actual resource utilization significantly exceeded planned capacity: Developers at 82% (vs planned 70%), QA at 24% (vs planned 20%), and DevOps at 14% (vs planned 10%). Higher workload due to defect fixes, increased retesting cycles, and production dependency management indicates resource planning gaps and potential team burnout risk.
Impact: Resource over-utilization increases burnout risk, reduces team morale, and may lead to quality degradation. Sustained high utilization levels are unsustainable and may cause attrition.

Risk Description: Project health status degraded from Green (Sprints 1-5) to Amber (Sprints 6-8) as documented in WSR reports. This degradation coincided with increased PR approval delays, access dependency issues, and production validation challenges. The cumulative effect of unresolved blockers and increasing defect counts triggered the status change.
Impact: Project health degradation signals accumulating issues requiring management intervention. Continued amber status may escalate to red, impacting delivery commitments and stakeholder confidence.

SLA Metric: Reopen count up to 2 per defect
Risk Title: High Defect Reopen Rate Indicating Quality Issues
Risk Description: Defect reopen counts reached up to 2 per defect across multiple sprints, indicating incomplete fixes and inadequate root cause analysis. Pattern analysis shows defects logged in Jira required 2-3 retesting cycles, with some defects reopened due to incomplete developer fixes. This suggests quality control gaps in the development and testing process.
Impact: High defect reopen rates indicate systemic quality issues, increase rework costs, extend delivery timelines, and erode customer confidence. Root cause analysis and process improvements are urgently needed.

Risk Title: Integration Validation Delays Due to Environment Dependencies
Risk Description: Integration validation tasks faced recurring delays due to environment access dependencies and infrastructure issues. Data flow checks, log analysis, and production monitoring activities were consistently impacted by access permission delays and environment readiness issues, causing tasks to remain in 'Blocked' or 'Resolved with delays' status.
Impact: Integration validation delays create risks in production deployment readiness, increase technical debt, and may cause data inconsistency issues. Timely resolution is critical for go-live success.

Financial Metric: Production support cost included in DevOps
SLA Metric: Certificate renewal and log access delays
Risk Description: Production monitoring and support activities faced recurring blockers including certificate renewal delays and production log access restrictions. Tasks remained in 'Blocked' or 'In Progress' status due to infrastructure dependencies. Bala's handover during Sprint 8 added transition risk during critical hypercare phase.
Impact: Production support bottlenecks create operational risks, delay incident resolution, and may impact system availability. Handover during hypercare increases knowledge transfer risk.

Risk Title: Budget Variance Concentration in QA and DevOps
Risk Description: While overall budget variance remained at 5% (within acceptable limits), the concentration of overruns in QA (₹25,000) and DevOps (₹20,000) indicates specific areas of underestimation. Development stayed within budget (₹40,000 under), but QA and DevOps exceeded planned costs due to increased retesting cycles and production monitoring requirements.
Impact: Concentrated budget overruns in specific areas indicate planning gaps and may signal ongoing cost escalation risk. Requires budget reforecasting and cost control measures.

Risk Description: Bala's planned leave and handover to Arun K during Sprint 8 hypercare phase creates knowledge transfer risk. Production monitoring and support responsibilities transitioned during critical stabilization period when system expertise is most needed. Backup coverage documented but handover timing increases operational risk.
Impact: Knowledge transfer during hypercare increases risk of incomplete handover, potential production issues, and reduced support quality. May impact incident resolution time and system stability.

Risk Description: Recurring access permission delays for UAT/prod environments indicate inefficient access management processes. Multiple tasks across all sprints faced blockers due to approval delays, impacting testing, validation, and production support activities. This suggests gaps in access governance and approval workflows.
Impact: Access management inefficiencies create compliance risks, delay project activities, and increase operational friction. Process improvements needed to streamline access provisioning and approvals.

Risk Description: Cutover rehearsal and go-live preparation activities faced blockers due to cutover checklist approval delays and interface freeze confirmation delays. Tasks remained blocked during critical Sprints 6-8 when go-live preparation should have been finalized. This creates risk of delayed production deployment.
Impact: Go-live readiness delays create deployment risk, may cause production cutover issues, and impact business continuity. Requires immediate management escalation and fast-track approval process.

SLA Metric: Sick leave, planned leave, flexi holidays
Risk Title: Team Continuity Risk Due to Leave Patterns
Risk Description: Multiple leave instances documented across sprints including sick leave (Nisha, Divya), planned leave (Bala, Ramesh K), flexi holidays (Priya M), and regular holidays (Keerthi L). While backup coverage was documented, frequent leave patterns during critical sprints may impact team continuity and knowledge availability.
Impact: Frequent leave patterns create knowledge gaps, may delay task completion, and increase dependency on backup resources. Requires robust knowledge sharing and cross-training.

SLA Metric: Multiple defect reopens and incomplete fixes
Risk Description: Pattern of incomplete bug fixes requiring multiple retest cycles and defect reopens indicates accumulating technical debt. Quick fixes without proper root cause analysis and inadequate code review during PR approval delays may have introduced shortcuts and workarounds that will require future remediation.
Impact: Accumulating technical debt increases future maintenance costs, reduces system reliability, and may cause production issues. Requires dedicated technical debt reduction sprints and improved code quality processes.
